text:
There once was a tiny mouse who lived in a cozy burrow next to a pond. Every morning she’d playfully swim across the pond, skipping stones. Once, while sliding back into the water, she heard a tiny voice.

“It wouldn’t mind?”

The mouse swam over. It turned out to be a tiny duckling with a shiny black head. It fluttered back home. That night it dreamed of having wings.

She started visiting the bog every day. She never flew; she just floated gently. There she found dozens of small creatures dwelling in the bog pond.

“Good evening,” she said softly.

Evening passed, and she became an interesting fixture. Every afternoon, a sleepy little toad would hop over.

“You’d never leave the water,” the toad would say. “Your mom thinks you’re way too tiny.” It chuckled.

“Oh, I wouldn’t say I never chase after crumbs. There’s always a tiny mouse cake slice hiding under that snail shell
